mappings.rationaleAI: The Second Sino-Japanese War (1937-1945) covers the Nanjing Massacre, Communist-Nationalist cooperation and conflict, US military aid, and Japan's surrender, directly matching A.3 on the war against Japan and B.2.b on Japan's alignment with Axis Powers.

The Cultural Revolution (1966-1976) covers Mao's mass mobilization, Red Guards, persecution of intellectuals, and the Gang of Four, directly matching A.5.c.iii on the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution.

The Long March (1934-35) covers the Red Army's retreat, the Zunyi Conference establishing Mao's leadership, and its transformation into a founding myth, directly matching A.2.d.ii on the Long March and Communist-Nationalist conflict.

The May Fourth Movement of 1919 covers student protests against the Treaty of Versailles, the New Culture Movement, and the founding of the Chinese Communist Party, directly matching A.1.b on modernization and the growth of nationalism.

Empress Dowager Cixi's management of late Qing modernization, the Hundred Days' Reform, and the Boxer Rebellion provide essential pre-revolutionary context for A.1.a on early power struggles and the background to the republic.
The Korean Empire (1897-1910) covers Japan's eventual annexation in 1910, directly matching C.1 on Japanese rule from 1910 to 1945.

The Russo-Japanese War's significance for the rise of Japan as a great power and its impact on Korea directly relates to B.1 on Japan's early 20th-century political developments and C.1 on the context of Japanese rule over Korea.
The Boxer Rebellion (1899-1901) and its aftermath contributed to the fall of imperial China and the conditions leading to the republic, providing context for A.1.a on early power struggles.
